content:
Begrijpelijk dat u dit wilt oplossen. Er kunnen een aantal zaken aan de hand zijn:

  • de accountinstellingen zijn niet meer up-to-date;
  • de serverinstellingen zijn niet meer up-to-date;
  • de firewall beperkt (tijdelijk) bepaalde diensten;
  • anti-virussoftware beperkt (tijdelijk) bepaalde diensten;
  • de internetverbinding is niet stabiel;
  • de e-mailprovider heeft beperkingen of is niet stabiel;
  • er is een add-on die dwars ligt.

Mogelijke oplossingen:

  • controleer de accountinstellingen;
  • controleer de serverinstellingen, zoals die door de provider worden opgegeven;
  • controleer de logging van de firewall en uw anti-virussoftware;
  • is de internetverbinding of de e-maildienst stabiel?;
  • test een en ander eens in De probleemoplossingsmodus van Thunderbird Probleemoplossingsmodus.
creator: Mozilla-assistent

content: il pc rallenta (e anche di brutto); ho seguito le procedure standard per la copia della cartella da vecchio pc a nuovo pc; dopodichè, le pec antecedenti al 01/12/25 non vengono più visualizzate (e questo dopo il passaggio dal vecchio pc windows 10 al nuovo 11); inoltre, le regole dei messaggi non "rispondono più ai comandi": i messaggi arrivano tutti nella posta in arrivo e non nelle sotto cartelle che ho creato (ad es., un messaggio Inps non va più nella cartella inps/inail, ma nella posta in arrivo o addirittura in altra cartella); quando è in esecuzione, thunderbird mangia letteralmente le risorse della RAM, rallentando tutto il sistema; nella casella "filtro veloce" posso mettere tutti i riferimenti che voglio, ma il messaggio non viene trovato e,. se viene trovato, viene visualizzato dopo circa 10 minuti

content:
What is your operating system?

What version of Thunderbird?

Who is your email provider?

Is this an IMAP account or POP3?

How long has this been going on?

What is your anti-virus, if any?

Error messages like this can have a few potential causes. The most common one is the anti-virus, which can take hold of your folder file and not let go. This can be tested by temporarily disabling the AV, by creating an exception for your Thunderbird Profile, or (assuming Windows) restarting the computer in Safe Mode with Networking, which temporarily disables the AV.

It could also be caused by a server problem, which could also account for you being repeatedly asked for your password. This is usually temporary and will go away when the server admins fix it.

There are also reports of similar errors caused by a corrupt message on the server. If you can narrow down the time frame when this started, you might use the provider's web mail interface to identify the bad message and delete it from there, which could allow downloads to happen again.
creator: rednoise

content:
What is your operating system?

How large is your Thunderbird Profile?

What are you trying to do with the old zip file?

If you're trying to import a backup made with Thunderbird's Export tool, there's a warning there that it doesn't work properly if your profile is larger than 2 GB, and instructs you how to manually back up the profile. Could this apply to you?

Can you open the ZIP file with Window's built-in zip feature or a 3rd-party utility like 7Zip?
creator: rednoise
